title: Fix updateTask/createTask input validation gaps (B27, B28, B29, B30, B31)
description:
**B27 (HIGH): updateTask() doesn't validate/sanitize inputs**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts:1370
- Unlike createTask() which sanitizes title/description/priority/definitionOfDone, updateTask() accepts raw Partial<Task> without any validation. Allows oversized titles, invalid priorities, negative reopenCount.
- Fix: Add sanitization in updateTask() for title (max 500), description (max 10000), priority (valid enum), reopenCount (non-negative integer), definitionOfDone (max 50 items, max 1000 chars each). Reuse existing sanitize* helpers from createTask.

**B28 (HIGH): createTask() accepts implementationPlan without validation**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts:1311
- implementationPlan array is accepted as-is without validating step structure, count limits, or field values.
- Fix: Validate implementationPlan: max 50 steps, each step must have description (string), status must be valid enum.

**B29 (MEDIUM): assignedWorkerId cleared on ALL status changes**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts:1406-1410
- When status changes and assignedWorkerId is not explicitly provided, it's set to null. This loses the audit trail of which worker completed a task (e.g. WORKING -> REVIEW).
- Fix: Only clear assignedWorkerId on specific transitions where it makes sense (e.g. back to BACKLOG/PLANNING). Preserve it on WORKING->REVIEW and REVIEW->DONE.

**B30 (MEDIUM): deleteTask() orphans child tasks**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts:1436-1471
- When a task with parentTaskId children is deleted, child tasks still reference the deleted parent.
- Fix: In deleteTask(), scan all tasks and clear parentTaskId where it matches the deleted taskId.

**B31 (MEDIUM): loadEntities() silently ignores corrupted files**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts:2110-2128
- Corrupted JSON files are silently skipped with empty catch block — no logging.
- Fix: Add logger.warn in the catch block with the filename and error message.
definitionOfDone: updateTask() validates title, description, priority, reopenCount, definitionOfDone
assignedWorkerId preserved on WORKING->REVIEW and REVIEW->DONE transitions
loadEntities() logs warnings for corrupted files
npm run build && npm test pass
Tests for validation rejecting invalid inputs

implementationPlan.description: B27 Fix: Add input sanitization to updateTask() in StateManager.ts (~line 1370). Before merging updates, sanitize provided fields using existing helpers: if `updates.title` is provided, call `sanitizeString(updates.title, 'title', 500)`. If `updates.description`, call `sanitizeString(updates.description, 'description', 10000)`. If `updates.priority`, validate against TaskPriority enum values. If `updates.reopenCount`, ensure it's a non-negative integer. If `updates.definitionOfDone`, run through `sanitizeStringArray()` with max 50 items, 1000 chars each. Build a `normalizedUpdates` object with sanitized values. Error handling: throw descriptive errors for invalid values (consistent with createTask).
implementationPlan.note: Added input sanitization to updateTask(): title (max 500), description (max 10000), priority (enum validation), reopenCount (non-negative integer), definitionOfDone (max 50 items, 1000 chars each), implementationPlan (via new sanitizeImplementationPlan helper). All using existing sanitize* helpers where possible.

implementationPlan.description: B28 Fix: Add implementationPlan validation to createTask() in StateManager.ts (~line 1311). Before accepting the plan array, validate: (1) max 50 steps, (2) each step must have a `description` string (non-empty), (3) if `status` is present it must be a valid step status ('PENDING', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED'). Add a helper `sanitizeImplementationPlan(plan: unknown[]): ImplementationStep[]` that strips invalid entries and truncates descriptions to 5000 chars. Also apply this validation in updateTask when implementationPlan is in the update payload. Error handling: invalid steps are stripped with a warning log, not rejected outright (to be lenient with agent input).
implementationPlan.note: Applied sanitizeImplementationPlan() to createTask(). Reuses the same helper added in step-1: max 50 steps, description required, status validated, affected/modified files capped at 50 each.

implementationPlan.description: B29 Fix: In updateTask() (~line 1406-1410), change the assignedWorkerId clearing logic. Instead of clearing on ALL status changes, only clear when transitioning to BACKLOG, PLANNING, or ARCHIVED. Define a set: `const CLEAR_WORKER_STATUSES = new Set(['BACKLOG', 'PLANNING', 'ARCHIVED'])`. Change the condition to: `const shouldClearWorker = statusChanged && CLEAR_WORKER_STATUSES.has(normalizedUpdates.status!) && normalizedUpdates.assignedWorkerId === undefined`. This preserves the worker reference on WORKING->REVIEW and REVIEW->DONE transitions. Error handling: no change needed.
implementationPlan.note: assignedWorkerId now only cleared on transitions to BACKLOG, PLANNING, or ARCHIVED. Preserved on WORKING->REVIEW and REVIEW->DONE to maintain audit trail.

implementationPlan.description: B30 Fix: In deleteTask() (~line 1436-1471), after deleting the task file and removing from memory, scan all remaining tasks for orphaned parentTaskId references. Add: `for (const t of this.tasks.values()) { if (t.parentTaskId === taskId) { const updated = { ...t, parentTaskId: null, updatedAt: new Date().toISOString() }; this.tasks.set(t.id, updated); await this.writeEntity('tasks', t.id, updated); } }`. Error handling: wrap in try/catch per child task so one failure doesn't block others.
implementationPlan.note: deleteTask now scans remaining tasks and clears parentTaskId references to deleted task. Each child update is wrapped in try/catch so one failure doesn't block others.

implementationPlan.description: B31 Fix: In loadEntities() (~line 2110-2128), replace the empty `catch {}` block with `catch (error) { logger.warn({ error, file: fullPath }, 'Failed to load entity file, skipping'); }`. This logs corrupted/invalid files so operators can diagnose data issues. Error handling: still skip the file (don't crash), just log.
implementationPlan.note: loadEntities() now logs a warning with file path and error for corrupted/invalid JSON files instead of silently ignoring them.

implementationPlan.description: Add tests: (1) updateTask with oversized title throws. (2) updateTask with invalid priority throws. (3) createTask with 60-step plan gets truncated to 50. (4) Verify assignedWorkerId preserved on WORKING->REVIEW transition. (5) Verify assignedWorkerId cleared on transition to BACKLOG. (6) deleteTask clears child parentTaskId. Run `npm run build && npm test`.
implementationPlan.note: Added 6 regression tests: updateTask truncates oversized title, rejects invalid priority, createTask truncates plan to 50 steps, assignedWorkerId preserved on WORKING->REVIEW, cleared on ->BACKLOG, deleteTask clears orphaned parentTaskId. 327 tests pass, build clean.
